enriched_points.expanded_content: Metal-based catalysts like noble metals (Au, Pd, Ag) excel in CO₂ reduction, while bio-inspired designs leverage natural mechanisms, yielding lower overpotentials and higher selectivity.
Hybrid catalysts combine metal and biomimetic components, enhancing efficiency and selectivity through synergistic interactions, as evidenced by recent studies on metal-organic frameworks.

Selectivity in CO₂ reduction varies significantly; metal catalysts favor CO and formate, while bio-inspired catalysts achieved remarkable selectivity for higher-value products, promoting sustainable pathways.

Stability of catalysts is critical; recent assessments indicate that bio-inspired catalysts maintain performance over extended periods, contrasting with the degradation observed in some metal-based counterparts.
